metadata.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04 — still elevated historically but showing first crack since December. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed is caught between stagflation pressures with no clear cut signal. Oil near $100/bbl. Positioning implies clients expect volatility but not a prolonged bear — net-buyers remain active in mega-cap tech on dips.

allocations.asset_strategy: Mega-cap AI infrastructure dip buyers (MSFT, AMZN, MU)
allocations.notes: MSFT and AMZN were top net-buys despite sharp drawdowns — clients buying dips on AI infrastructure thesis. MSFT down 25% YTD, NVDA near 200-day MA support. Broadcom (AVGO) top net-sell signals rotation within tech. AMD also net-sold.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.notes: Industrials led all sectors in net-positive flows in March. War in Iran driving defense and infrastructure demand. Aligns with existing IFRA position in Bill's portfolio.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.asset_strategy: Rate pause beneficiaries, broad financial sector exposure
allocations.notes: Financials saw second-best net-positive sector flows. Fed paused for second consecutive meeting at 3.5–3.75% target range. Rate stability benefits banks and diversified financials. COF and CFG already in portfolio.

Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.asset_strategy: Selective dip exposure; Tesla as speculative recovery play
allocations.notes: Third in net-positive sector flows. TSLA returned to top 5 net-buys at $360–$380 support. Consumer sentiment weakened (payrolls -92K vs estimates). Risk is stagflation eroding consumer spending. Confidence low given macro headwinds.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.notes: Consumer staples saw net-positive flows — consistent with risk-off rotation pattern. Historically outperforms during geopolitical shocks and inflationary environments. Underweighted in Bill's current portfolio — potential displacement opportunity.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.asset_strategy: Rate pause REIT exposure, diversification vs liquid portfolio
allocations.notes: Real estate saw net-positive flows. Fed rate pause at 3.5–3.75% relieves some pressure on rate-sensitive REITs. Note: Bill's RE portfolio provides direct real estate exposure — liquid REIT allocation should be sized with total wealth in mind to avoid double-counting.

Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.notes: Health care saw net-positive flows in March. Defensive characteristic suits current geopolitical uncertainty. Bill already has significant exposure (UNH 9% of portfolio, XBI, XLV). Maintain rather than add aggressively given UNH concentration.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.asset_strategy: War premium oil play; trim OXY-style producers, hold pipeline/midstream
allocations.notes: Energy saw net-negative flows as clients took profits on war-driven price spike (OXY top 5 net-sell). Oil near $100/bbl. Prefer midstream/infrastructure (ET already held) over pure E&P for income stability. XOM position offers both yield and war premium.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.notes: Fed paused at 3.5–3.75% but faces stagflation crosswinds. PPI rose far above expectations in February. Duration risk elevated. Short-duration income ETFs like JPIE remain attractive. Avoid long-duration Treasuries given inflationary pressure from oil spike.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.asset_strategy: Developed markets hedge; avoid EM war exposure
allocations.notes: STAX doesn't surface international flows directly. VEA (developed markets) and EWZ/EEM (EM) in existing portfolio. Iran war increases geopolitical risk in EM. Developed markets (VEA, EFG) preferred. BABA exposure adds China risk — monitor.
allocations.executive_summary: Risk-off rotation underway as Iran war shock drives clients toward diversified ETFs over individual names. STAX fell 2.23% to 56.04. Info tech saw the biggest net outflows despite prior strength; industrials, financials, consumer discretionary led inflows. Fed caught between stagflation pressures.

allocations.asset_strategy: Elevated cash buffer; money market at ~4.5% yield
allocations.notes: S&P 500 fell 7%+ in March — worst since September 2022. Maintaining elevated cash buffer is prudent for opportunistic deployment. Money market at ~4.5% is competitive. STAX at 56 suggests clients expect volatility to abate, but geopolitical uncertainty justifies dry powder.
